meta.sprintTitle: Admin Console v1 - Users + Project Lookup
meta.goal: Enable authorised admins to securely search, inspect, and troubleshoot users and their storybook projects from the Admin Console without direct database access.

basedOnPriorSprints: GA4 funnel and drop-off analysis planning
Error handling and graceful recovery planning
whyNow: Support and launch operations need fast visibility into who the user is, what project state they are in, and where they may be stuck
Admin users need safe read-first tooling before more powerful operational modules like export retry or billing overrides
User and project lookup is the base for support, debugging, moderation, and analytics investigation

nonGoals: Editing customer story content directly from admin
Direct deletion of user accounts or projects
Bulk operations across large sets of users/projects
dependencies: Sprint 45 admin auth and RBAC foundation
Chapter/page or equivalent project structure tables if available

operational: Support admin can locate a user within a few seconds using email or name
Admin can view linked projects and their high-level state without DB access
Admin can identify common stuck states such as onboarding incomplete, draft only, export failed, or inactive
security: 0 non-admin users can access admin user/project data
0 admin roles without permission can access restricted data views
Sensitive fields are masked or excluded by default
quality: 100% of user/project admin endpoints are permission-guarded server-side
User search and project detail views are covered by automated tests
Audit events exist for sensitive record views where required

personas.name: Super Admin
personas.description: Can view all admin-supported user and project data.

personas.name: Support Admin
personas.description: Primary operator for support cases, troubleshooting user journeys and project state.

personas.name: Content Admin
personas.description: May need limited project visibility for moderation or content-quality review, but not full account-sensitive data.

personas.name: Normal App User
personas.description: Regular Memorioso customer. Must never access admin lookup screens.

inScope: Admin users list with search and filters
Admin project detail page with read-only support metadata
High-level onboarding and activity state visibility
Audit logging for sensitive admin record access where needed

architecture.recommendedApproach: Build read-only admin lookup tools on top of Sprint 45 RBAC. Expose dedicated admin APIs that join user and project metadata into support-safe response models. Keep admin views summary-first, with drill-down pages for specific users and projects.
principles: Mask sensitive data where not required
Design for support speed and clarity rather than full internal raw table dumps

dataExposureRules: users.view is required for user list and user detail pages
projects.view is required for project detail page access
support.view is required for support-specific metadata like onboarding state, last active, client error summary, or export state summary
content.moderate may unlock visibility of moderation flags or content review markers where implemented

sourceEntities.name: projects
sourceEntities.purpose: Storybook or memorioso book entities linked to users

sourceEntities.name: project_versions_or_pages
sourceEntities.purpose: Optional aggregated counts for support visibility

sourceEntities.name: export_jobs
sourceEntities.purpose: Optional summary fields for latest export status if available

sourceEntities.name: audit_logs
sourceEntities.purpose: Trace admin access and support review actions

userStories.title: Search users in admin console
userStories.iWant: to search by email or name
userStories.soThat: I can quickly find the customer who contacted support

userStories.title: Inspect a user profile
userStories.iWant: to view account and onboarding status plus linked projects
userStories.soThat: I can understand where the user is stuck

userStories.title: Inspect a project
userStories.iWant: to view read-only project state and summary metadata
userStories.soThat: I can diagnose issues like incomplete setup or failed export preparation

userStories.title: Role-limited data access
userStories.iWant: to access only project-level moderation-safe information
userStories.soThat: I do not see unrelated sensitive account data

userStories.title: Support-safe project lookup
userStories.iWant: admins to troubleshoot through UI instead of database access

functionalRequirements.title: Admin users list
requirements: Provide /admin/users page
Display paginated list of users
Allow search by email, full name, or user id
Allow filtering by account status, onboarding status, plan tier if available, and activity recency
List view shows summary metadata useful for support

functionalRequirements.title: Admin user detail page
requirements: Provide /admin/users/:userId page
Show base user profile summary
Show onboarding state and last active timestamp
Show list of linked projects with project status and last updated timestamp
Do not expose secrets, tokens, or private raw auth payloads

functionalRequirements.title: Admin project detail page
requirements: Provide /admin/projects/:projectId page
Show owner summary and project summary
Show project lifecycle state such as draft, in progress, export ready, exported, failed if available
Show counts such as pages, chapters, assets if available
Show latest export summary and latest known client/system issue summary if available
Keep page read-only in this sprint

functionalRequirements.title: Permission-aware data views
requirements: Only roles with users.view can access user list and user details
Only roles with projects.view can access project detail
Only roles with support.view can view support-sensitive metadata
Content admins may access project moderation metadata without seeing unrelated user account detail

functionalRequirements.title: Linking between user and project views
requirements: User detail page links to project detail pages where allowed
Project detail page links back to owner user page only where users.view exists
Navigation must respect permissions and not surface broken links

functionalRequirements.title: Search and filtering
requirements: Search should support partial matches for common support use cases
Filtering should be combinable with search
Sorting should support last active, signup date, and last updated where available

functionalRequirements.title: Read-only support-safe inspection
requirements: No edit controls for user or project records in this sprint
Dangerous actions like delete, restore, export retry, or billing override must not appear
UI should clearly present this module as inspection-only

functionalRequirements.title: Audit logging
requirements: Log access to user detail pages
Log access to project detail pages
Log search queries only if done safely and in privacy-conscious form
Include admin actor, target resource id, timestamp, and action

functionalRequirements.title: Usability for support workflows
requirements: Empty state should explain no results found
Error state should not leak internals
Loading state should be clear and fast
Important statuses should be visually scannable

pages.title: Users
pages.purpose: Search and browse Memorioso users with support-safe summary metadata.

pages.title: User Detail
pages.purpose: Inspect a specific user and their linked projects.

pages.title: Project Detail
pages.purpose: Inspect read-only project status and support metadata.

uxRules: Use badges for statuses like active, disabled, onboarding incomplete, export failed
Do not overload the screen with raw JSON or internal-only fields
Make owner and project relationships obvious
Keep the module visually consistent with Admin Console shell from Sprint 45

apiSurface.note: Adapt naming to existing backend conventions.

endpoints.purpose: Search and list users with pagination and filters.
Return user detail and linked project summaries.
Return read-only project detail for admin inspection.

error.message: You do not have permission to access this resource.

technicalTasks.title: Design admin read models
technicalTasks.description: Define support-safe DTOs/view models for user and project lookup responses.

technicalTasks.title: Build admin users list API
technicalTasks.description: Implement paginated, searchable, filterable user list endpoint with permission checks.

technicalTasks.title: Build admin user detail API
technicalTasks.description: Return user summary plus linked project summaries, respecting role-based data exposure.

technicalTasks.title: Build admin project detail API
technicalTasks.description: Return read-only support metadata for a project with optional aggregated counts and latest status summaries.
deliverables: safe fallbacks when optional data is absent

technicalTasks.title: Create users list UI
technicalTasks.description: Build searchable users table with filters, pagination, and status badges.

technicalTasks.title: Create user detail UI
technicalTasks.description: Build user detail page with account summary and linked project list.

technicalTasks.title: Create project detail UI
technicalTasks.description: Build project detail page showing support-safe project metadata and status panels.

technicalTasks.title: Audit log integration
technicalTasks.description: Log admin access to user and project detail resources.

technicalTasks.title: Permission and masking rules
technicalTasks.description: Implement server-side field inclusion/exclusion based on role and permission.

technicalTasks.title: Testing
technicalTasks.description: Add unit, integration, and E2E coverage for user/project lookup flows.

allowedExamples: Email for support/admin roles where justified
Counts such as pages or chapters
Latest export state summary if available
restrictedExamples: Sensitive billing details beyond plan tier label
Private story content body text unless a future moderation sprint explicitly requires it
maskingRules: For list views, prefer summary and partial exposure over full raw details
Expose full email only where role and support use case justify it
Hide internal storage keys, signed URLs, and secrets

supportStatesToSurface.description: User has signed up but not completed essential onboarding
User account exists but no storybook created
User has a draft project not yet progressed toward export
Latest known export-related activity indicates failure
User has not been active for a configurable period

metrics: average response time for users list and detail APIs
loggingNotes: Avoid storing overly broad personal data in general logs
If logging search terms, consider truncation or hashing strategy depending on compliance posture
Prefer audit logs for traceability and app logs for technical debugging

securityRequirements: All lookup endpoints must be protected by Sprint 45 admin auth middleware and permission checks
Client-side hidden routes are not sufficient; server must reject unauthorised access
No raw database dumps or unrestricted query builders exposed to admin UI
Sensitive fields must be excluded or masked by serializer layer
Admin list/detail pages must remain noindex and excluded from public sitemap
Access denied responses must not confirm record existence to unauthorised actors
Project and user IDs should be validated before query execution

acceptanceCriteria.when: they open /admin/users and search by a customer email
acceptanceCriteria.then: matching users are returned with support-safe summary fields

acceptanceCriteria.when: they open a user detail page
acceptanceCriteria.then: they can see onboarding status, last activity, and linked project summaries

acceptanceCriteria.when: they open a project detail page
acceptanceCriteria.then: they can see read-only project status, counts, and latest support metadata if available

acceptanceCriteria.when: they attempt to access /admin/users or its API
they access an allowed project detail page
acceptanceCriteria.then: they only see fields permitted for their role

acceptanceCriteria.when: they attempt to call any admin lookup API

acceptanceCriteria.given: an admin views a user or project detail page
acceptanceCriteria.then: an audit log entry is created with actor, resource, and timestamp

acceptanceCriteria.given: no matching users exist for a search
acceptanceCriteria.then: the UI shows a clear no-results state without errors

unitTests: role-based serializer excludes restricted fields correctly
search parameter parsing and filter mapping work correctly
status badge mapping covers all known states
integrationTests: content_admin receives masked/limited payload where allowed
e2eTests: support_admin searches user and opens detail page
support_admin opens linked project detail page
empty state shown when no users match
negativeTests: invalid user id returns safe not-found response
invalid project id returns safe not-found response
unauthorised actor cannot infer whether resource exists
forged client permission does not bypass server serializer or guard

risks.risk: Too much sensitive data shown in admin read views
risks.mitigation: Use explicit allow-list serializers and role-based field exposure

risks.risk: Support tooling becomes a raw internal dump and hard to use
risks.mitigation: Design summary-first pages with clear statuses and only useful metadata

risks.risk: Search becomes slow as user base grows
risks.mitigation: Add indexes for searchable fields and paginate aggressively

risks.risk: Project state is inconsistent across tables
risks.mitigation: Define clear derived status rules and document precedence

recommendedOrder: Define admin DTOs and field exposure policy
Build user detail and project detail UIs
futureSprintHooks: Sprint 48: Billing and plan support view
Sprint 49: Feedback, moderation, and support inbox
Sprint 50: Feature flags and operational controls

definitionOfDone: Admin users list is available with search, filters, and pagination
Admin user detail page shows support-safe account summary and linked projects
Admin project detail page shows read-only support metadata
All related APIs are permission-guarded server-side
Role-based data masking/exposure rules are enforced
Audit logging exists for sensitive admin record views
Automated tests cover happy paths and denial paths
Documentation explains available fields and intended support usage
